LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MILLENNIUM PARTNERS, MILLENNIUM PARTNERS SPORTS CLUB MANAGEMENT LLC. AND ERIKA VARGAS' DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT, ON ITS OWN MOTION THE COURT SUSTAINS ITS OWN DEMURRER WITH THIRTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION THAT ARE THE SUBJECT OF DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER ON THE GROUNDS THAT THE 85 PAGE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT VIOLATES CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 425.010(A)(1) WHICH REQUIRES THAT A COMPLAINT CONTAIN "A STATEMENT OF FACTS CONSTITUTING THE CAUSE OF ACTION, IN ORDINARY AND CONCISE LANGUAGE." THE SHEER LENGTH OF THE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT MAKES IT VIRTUALLY IMPOSSIBLE TO DETERMINE IF ANY OF THE CLAIMS ALLEGED THEREIN ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO COMPLY WITH SECTION 425.010. COURT TO PREPARE AND MAIL OUT A PROPOSED ORDER TO PARTIES. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/HK)
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE OF SEP-13-2016 IS OFF CALENDAR. DISMISSAL ON FILE. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

Off Calendar Apr-08-2016 Continued To Jun-22-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-09-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS MILLENNIUM PARTNERS, MILLENNIUM PARTNERS SPORTS CLUB MANAGEMENT LLC. AND ERIKA VARGAS' DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT, on its own motion the court sustains its own demurrer with thirty days leave to amend as to all causes of action that are the subject of defendants' demurrer on the grounds that the 85 page fourth amended complaint violates Code of Civil Procedure 425.010(a)(1) which requires that a complaint contain "A statement of facts constituting the cause of action, in ordinary and concise language." The sheer length of the fourth amended complaint makes it virtually impossible to determine if any of the claims alleged therein are adequately pled. Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to comply with section 425.010. Court to prepare and mail out a proposed order to parties. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/HK)

Joint Notice Of Motion And Motion To Continue The Trial Date
Off Calendar Jan-21-2016 Text Ruling

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-09-2015 Continued To Feb-08-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-30-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. DIANE JOSEPHS OF JOSEPHS & BLUM, TEL. NO. 1-415-544-0400 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MATTHEW A. GOODIN OF EPSTEIN BECKER & GREEN, TEL. NO. 1-415-399-6021 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS MILLENNIUM PARTNERS, MILLENNIUM PARTNERS SPORTS CLUB MANAGEMENT LLC. and ERIKA VARGAS' DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT - Demurrer sustained with leave to amend in part and denied in part. Defendant's demurrer to the first and eighth causes of action for wrongful discharge in violation of public policy is sustained with ten days leave to amend to add the specific OSHA violations which Plaintiffs allude to in the complaint. Defendant's demurrer to the tenth cause of action, which pertains to violation of Labor Code section 6310 is also sustained with ten days leave to amend to add the specific OSHA violations. Plaintiffs have not and are unable to plead facts which allow them to bring a claim under Labor Code section 1102.5(a-c). At the time of Plaintiffs' employment, Labor Code section 1102.5(a-c) required reporting to an outside agency which is not alleged in the complaint. Thus, Defendant's demurrer to the second and ninth causes of action is sustained with leave to amend. Defendant's demurrer to the third cause of action for sexual harassment and a hostile work environment is sustained with ten days leave to amend to specify the pervasiveness and severity of the alleged harassment and to plead specific facts regarding the hostility that resulted from the harassment. Defendant's demurrer to the fourth cause of action is sustained with ten days leave to amend because Plaintiff's allegations of sexual harassment are deficient. Defendant's demurrer to the fifth cause of action is sustained with ten days leave to amend. Because Plaintiff Ludwigsen has been granted with leave to plead facts regarding the sexual harassment and discrimination, defendant's demurrer to the fifth cause of action for failure to prevent discrimination, harassment, and retaliation is also sustained with ten days leave to amend. Defendant's demurrer to the sixth and eleventh causes of action is sustained with ten days leave to amend in order to plead that proper notice was provided under Labor Code section 2699.3(a)(1). The demurrer to the seventh and sixteenth causes of action is sustained with ten days leave to amend in order to meet the class action requirements. Defendant's demurrer to the twelfth cause of action is sustained with tens days leave to amend in order to allow Plaintiff Ludwigsen to add specific facts showing extreme emotional suffering. Defendant's demurrer to the thirteenth, fourteenth, and fifteenth causes of action is denied. Plaintiff Ludwigsen's deposition statements cannot be considered in Defendant's demurrer. Defendant's demurrer to the seventeenth and eighteenth causes of action is denied. Plaintiff Ludwigsen filed her claims within the statute of limitations. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ajr
